I have found the root cause. The data skew report shows:
- Total records: 100,000
- Distinct user_ids: 1,000
- Hot user_id: 999
- Hot user records: 50,000 (50% of all records)
- Skew ratio: 500x (far above the 10x threshold)

The diagnostic server also confirms Stage 3 GROUP BY user_id triggers OutOfMemoryError.

Now let me look at the ETL job YAML to understand the current configuration and plan the fix.

2	kind: Job
3	metadata:
4	  name: etl-job
5	  namespace: spark-etl
6	spec:
7	  backoffLimit: 0
8	  template:
9	    metadata:
10	      labels:
11	        app: etl-job
12	    spec:
13	      serviceAccountName: spark
14	      restartPolicy: Never
15	      containers:
16	      - name: spark-etl
17	        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
18	        command:
19	        - /bin/bash
20	        - -c
21	        - |
22	          set -e
23	          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
24	          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
25	          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
26	          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
27	          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
28	          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
29	          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
30	          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
31	            --master local[2] \
32	            --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \
33	            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
34	            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
35	            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
36	            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
37	            500
38	          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
39	        resources:
40	          requests:
41	            cpu: 200m
42	            memory: 512Mi
43	          limits:
44	            cpu: 500m
45	            memory: 2Gi

file.content:
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: Job
metadata:
  name: etl-job
  namespace: spark-etl
spec:
  backoffLimit: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: etl-job
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: spark
      restartPolicy: Never
      containers:
      - name: spark-etl
        image: apache/spark:3.5.3
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          export SPARK_LOCAL_IP=$(hostname -i)
          echo "Stage 1: load raw data"
          echo "Stage 2: clean and transform"
          echo "Stage 3: aggregate by user_id (skewed on user_id 999)"
          # --executor-memory 256m is too small for the skewed Stage 3 aggregation
          # and triggers OutOfMemoryError. The agent must raise it (e.g. 1g),
          # scale workers, or enable AQE skew handling before re-running.
          /opt/spark/bin/spark-submit \
            --master local[2] \
            --conf spark.executor.memory=1g \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.enabled=true \
            --conf spark.sql.adaptive.skewJoin.enabled=true \
            --class org.apache.spark.examples.SparkPi \
            /opt/spark/examples/jars/spark-examples_2.12-3.5.3.jar \
            500
          echo "spark_etl_pipeline_verified"
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 200m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 2Gi
